title: Azure OpenAI endpoint public + prompt logged to App Insights
narrative: An Azure OpenAI endpoint is reachable publicly, and its consuming app streams every prompt/response to Application Insights. Anyone with Reader on the App Insights resource can read every prompt — including prompts containing customer PII, internal credentials in RAG context, or confidential business queries.

steps.action: Send a prompt containing sensitive business data.
Query requests table; read every prompt / completion body.
steps.gain: Mass PII / secret leak from LLM traffic.

title: Cognitive Services key sprawl via multiple account copies
narrative: Cognitive Services subscription keys are distributed via email, Confluence, and shared password managers to every team that wants LLM access. Keys never rotate; there's no way to trace which team leaked which key.

steps.action: Search Slack / SharePoint for 'Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key'.
Use key for free inference — billed to the owning tenant.

title: ML Workspace compute instance with public IP + SSH
narrative: Azure ML Workspace provisions compute instances with public IP and SSH enabled. The compute instance holds a managed identity with Contributor on the workspace — meaning any shell access yields access to every dataset, model registry, and endpoint in the workspace.

steps.action: SSH to ML compute instance; brute-force or use leaked key.
Use workspace MI to read datastores + export models.

title: Bot Service app registration over-privileged
narrative: An Azure Bot Service registration has application permissions including User.Read.All and Channel.ReadAll — far more than needed for its normal Q&A chatbot function. A compromise of the bot's service principal yields tenant-wide read of user directory data.

steps.action: Compromise the bot's App Service via any web vuln.
Call /users and /channels via Graph; exfil directory.

title: Azure AI Search with public access + admin API key leak
narrative: Azure AI Search service has public network access and admin API keys that never rotate. A leaked admin key grants full read/write on every index — including ability to drop indexes, add poisoned content, or export entire search corpora.

steps.action: Find admin API key in an app's appsettings.json / GitHub.
Enumerate indexes; export every document.

title: OpenAI deployment with no content filter + jailbreak leakage
narrative: An Azure OpenAI deployment has content filters disabled (or set to lowest) AND is publicly reachable. Attackers extract system prompts, harvest RAG context, and use the endpoint as a free LLM relay — all invoices paid by the victim.

steps.action: Send jailbreak prompt to extract the system prompt.
steps.gain: Internal prompt + any RAG data embedded in context.

steps.action: Relay arbitrary prompts through the endpoint; bill paid by victim.
steps.gain: Free LLM access + potential reputation harm from outputs.

blast_radius.max_privilege: System prompt + RAG leak; compute abuse.

title: ML datastore with cleartext credential in datastore config
narrative: An Azure ML datastore references a SQL database using plaintext username/password in the datastore registration JSON. Anyone with Reader on the workspace can dump this config.

steps.action: GET /datastores; read credential field.
Connect to DB directly.

title: Computer Vision endpoint without customer-managed key
narrative: Computer Vision processes images containing PII (passports, licenses) but stores them with Microsoft-managed keys. A court order or Microsoft internal compromise could expose historical processed images.

steps.action: Data processed without customer-controlled key.
Subpoena or insider access yields plaintext.

title: Document Intelligence with public access + PII forms
narrative: Document Intelligence ingests forms with SSN, banking details, health records. The service has public network access and a leaked API key works from anywhere. Attackers call the endpoint with victim-organisation images to process their own forms through the victim's quota.

steps.action: Scrape API key from victim's mobile app bundle.
Process attacker-forms through victim's quota; extract model outputs.
steps.gain: Free PII extraction paid by victim.

title: ML pipeline with hardcoded secret in code artifact
narrative: An ML training script in the workspace repo hardcodes a storage account key for dataset access. The training image built from the repo embeds this key; anyone with pull access to the container registry has the key.

steps.action: Pull the training image; docker history reveals the key.
Authenticate against the target storage.

title: Azure Bot managed identity with over-broad Graph scope
narrative: An Azure Bot runs in Teams and the bot's service principal has Chat.Read.All and Files.Read.All delegated — the developer couldn't figure out per-conversation scopes. A compromised bot becomes a tenant-wide Teams eavesdropper.

steps.action: Exploit bot endpoint or SP credential.
Call Graph to read any chat in any tenant the bot joined.

data_at_risk: Every Teams chat the bot has access to

title: Azure AI Content Safety bypassed via token replay
narrative: Content Safety screens LLM outputs, but the moderation verdict is cached client-side for replay. An attacker captures a 'safe' verdict and replays it for different content, bypassing moderation.

steps.action: Observe moderation verdict traffic; capture 'safe' signatures.
Submit harmful content, present cached token; app accepts.

title: Translator service with embedded API key in mobile app
narrative: A mobile app ships with the Translator subscription key in its APK/IPA. Anyone decompiling the app obtains the key, using victim's free tier for their own translation bill.

steps.action: Decompile the app; extract the subscription key.
Use key to run high-volume translation workloads.

title: ML Workspace without encryption-at-rest CMK
narrative: ML Workspace stores training artifacts with Microsoft-managed keys. Regulatory frameworks (FedRAMP High, HIPAA-regulated PHI) require CMK. A compliance finding turns into a mandatory remediation + potential audit failure.

steps.action: Identify ML workspace without CMK.
Training data accessed via platform-level mechanism.

title: Azure AI Search RAG corpus with PII and unauth read
narrative: An LLM RAG application uses Azure AI Search to retrieve internal documents including customer PII. The search service has public endpoint access and query API keys that are distributed broadly. Anyone with a query key can enumerate the corpus and exfiltrate PII.

steps.action: Obtain a query key from a shared secret store.
Enumerate documents via wildcard queries; export PII.
steps.gain: Mass PII breach via RAG corpus.
